titlestmt.titleproper: Register to The South Before the War Company Papers
South Before the War Company Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Ana Bentancur

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 8194277.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to local Music Library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 3, 2020 at 00:25

did.unittitle: The South Before the War Company Papers
did.abstract: Music and other materials from the touring minstrel show The South Before the War

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open to researchers by appointment. There are no restricted materials in the collection. Please contact the Special Collections staff to schedule an appointment.
Some of the materials may be stored at the Library's off-campus shelving facility, so researchers should allow at least two business days to have the appropriate boxes paged.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The South Before the War Company Papers were established in the Music Library of Yale University by Marguerite S. Megaw in 1981.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The South Before the War Company Papers are the physical property of the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University. Copyrights belong to the composers and authors, or their legal heirs and assigns.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 87, The South Before the War Company Papers in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: In 2 series, as follows: I. Publicity, scripts, and other materials. II. Music.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The South Before the War Company Papers include scripts, orchestral parts, published and unpublished sheet music, and other ephemera. Unfortunately, there is no list of musical numbers used in the show, and it is difficult to judge if any of the orchestral parts constitute a complete score. The script is complete, however, and it is possible to combine that with the songs and segues that most frequently appear in the orchestral parts to make a very preliminary sketch of the musical numbers involved:
Chorus at curtain: Negro melodies: "Picking Cotton", "Oaken Bucket" etc.
"The Days When I Was Young" (or "My Old Savannah Home"?)
"The Man Who Led the Cake Walk"

The Papers, of course, include many more songs than indicated in this list. The individual orchestral scores are largely made up of segues identified only by script cues, and there is some variation from score to score. The second half of the show appears to have been only loosely related to the story line: it is basically a variety revue.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The South Before the War Company was a minstrel show owned and managed by Herman Wallum (alias Harry Martell) that toured the United States in the late 1890s (and possibly before and after that time as well). The musical show performed by the Company was also named The South Before the War, and the company-owned parlor car bore the same name.

Although minstrel shows were perhaps the most popular genre of musical theater in America during the late 19th century, few are as thoroughly documented as The South Before the War. These papers provide an important glimpse into the musical practices and social attitudes of that era.

odd.head: General
odd.p: 1 small publicity brochure
Photograph, b&w, of company & private parlor car
Publicity statement, handwritten, in ink -- 3 pages
Contract for performance at Atheneum Theatre [city not mentioned] dated 1898. [The contract is the only dated document in this collection.]
List of company expenses, handwritten, in ink
Log sheet, unused, for company's itinerary

did.unittitle: "Harry Martell's New and Original Picturesque Spectacle Entitled 'South Before the War' in Three Acts and Numerous Tableaux"

odd.head: General
odd.p: Complete typed script -- 28 pages

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph [Harry Martell's handwriting?], in ink -- 13 pages
4 typed scripts -- 17 pages, 16 pages, 24 pages, 24 pages

odd.head: General
odd.p: Handwritten script, in ink -- 27 pages
Handwritten script for scene 1, Act II -- 14 pages
Typed script -- 20 pages

odd.head: General
odd.p: [fragments, duplicates, etc.]

odd.head: General
odd.p: [no author given]
One-page orchestral parts for: 1st violin, 2nd violin, viola, bass, flute, clarinet in B, cornet in B, trombone, drums

odd.head: General
odd.p: [no author given]
One-page orchestral parts for: 1st violin, 2nd violin, viola, cello, bass, flute, clarinet, cornet in A, trombone, drums

odd.head: General
odd.p: [by E. Morback Buck?]
One-page orchestral parts for: leader, 2nd violin, viola, bass, flute, clarinet in B flat, cornet in B flat, trombone, drums

odd.head: General
odd.p: [no author given]
Mimeographed one-page orchestral parts for: 1st violin, 2nd violin, viola, bass, flute, clarinet in A, cornet in A, drums & bells

odd.head: General
odd.p: In manuscript form, in ink and pencil

did.unittitle: "Who Dat Say Chicken in dis Crowd"

odd.head: General
odd.p: Published orchestral parts for (one page each): 1st violin, 2nd violin, viola, bass & cello, flute, clarinet in A, cornet in A, trombone

odd.head: General
odd.p: Published orchestral parts (one page each) for: 1st violin, 2nd violin, viola, cello & bass, flute, clarinet in A (2 copies), cornet in A, trombone, drums

odd.head: General
odd.p: Published orchestral parts (one page each) for: 2nd violin, bass & cello, flute, clarinet in A, cornet in A, trombone, drums

odd.head: General
odd.p: Published orchestral parts (one page each) for: piano, 1st violin, 2nd violin, viola, bass, flute, clarinet in B flat, 1st cornet in B flat, 2nd cornet in B flat, 1st & 2nd trombone, trombone, drums

did.unittitle: "I'm the Warmest Member in the Land"

odd.head: General
odd.p: [incomplete]
Published orchestral parts (one page each) for: cello & bass, cornet in A, trombone, drums

odd.head: General
odd.p: (march)
Published orchestral parts (one page each) for: piano, cello, 2nd clarinet in B flat, oboe, 1st horn in F, 2nd horn in F, 2nd cornet in B flat, bassoon

odd.head: General
odd.p: [incomplete]
Published orchestral parts (one page each) for: 2nd violin, viola, bass, flute, clarinet in B flat, cornet in B flat, trombone, drums

Published orchestral parts (one page each) for: 2nd violin, bass, clarinet in B flat, cornet in B flat

odd.head: General
odd.p: All for you (by Carl Vandal) -- 3 pages
As it was of old (Lyn Udall) -- 3 pages
The cake-walk in the sky (Ben Harney) -- 4 pages
The change will do you good (John W. Bratton) -- 3 pages
Chicoongo (Arthur S. Shaw) -- 3 pages
City of Harrisburg (L. A. Drumheller) -- 1 page
Coming Home to Die (Gussie L. Davis) -- 3 pages
Coons in the Canebrake (M. S. Clayson) -- 4 pages
Could the dream but go on (Richard Stahl) -- 3 pages
The dandy sparrow cops (W. H. Batchelor) -- 4 pages
Dear old soul (John W. Bratton) -- 4 pages
Done said all I had to say (J. Hicks & S. Perrin) -- 4 pages
Everything's a comin' mah way (E. Moulton) -- 3 pages
The field burglars (G. H. Bramhall) -- 4 pages
Flanagan, rushin' the can again (Taylor & Stephens) -- 4 pages
The gripcar gripman's grip (Wm. Devere) -- 4 pages
He is coming to us dead (Gussie L. Davis) -- 4 pages
I'm not particular (Murray & Leigh) -- 4 pages
I've got money locked up in a vault (I. Jones) -- 3 pages
I think of thee (Louis C. Elson & Hartel) -- 2 pages
In the heart of old New England (T. S. Allen) -- 3 pages
Just a little spray of heather-bells (Heelan & Hamilton) -- 3 pages
Kiss your goosie woosie (C. G. Cotes & B. Scott) & It didn't take long to do (Murray & Leigh) -- 6 pages
Little Miss Vixen (Louis Reinhard) -- 4 pages
A long time ago (C. Moreland) -- 4 pages
Ma Caroline (Lee Johnson) -- 4 pages
A Maine girl (W. E. Chadwick) -- 4 pages
Mamie Tracy (Roger Harding) & Mary Had a Little Lamb (J. Tabrar) -- 6 pages
Mandy, will you be my lady love? (S. L. Perrin) -- 4 pages
Meet me half way (J. Donigan) -- 3 pages
Miss Virginia (Jefferson & Irving) -- 4 pages
Molly McEntee (Edwin W. Cole) -- 4 pages
My little octoroon (W. R. Williams) -- 3 pages
My old Westchester home among the maples (W. B. Gray) -- 4 pages (2 copies)
Oft-times (J. E. Fay & J. B. Oliver) -- 4 pages
Oh! such a business! (Arthur Gillespie & G. Edwards) -- 4 pages
Only a dream (J. E. Fay & J. B. Oliver) -- 3 pages
The pride of the pier (W. H. Ford & J. W. Bratton) -- 3 pages
The sermon Deacon Vandewater preached (W.B. Gray) -- 4 pages
Sweet, sweet love (C. Horwitz & F. V. Bowers) -- 3 pages
Take good care of mother ('Murphy' & J. A. Bland) -- 4 pages
There's always a home for you (G. L. Davis) -- 4 pages
There's something going to happen mighty soon (C. Lane) -- 4 pages
Unrequited (W. Blake & G. J. Couchois) -- 3 pages
Why don't you love me in the same old way? (W.R. Williams) -- 3 pages
Cantiniere (W. L. Gilmore) & The best in the wide, wide, world (Cooper & Ryan) & Turtle dance (G. A. Held) & A fancied resemblance, that's all (Browne & Coleman) -- 13 pages

odd.head: General
odd.p: America my glorious home - for trombone -- 1/2 page
Break the news - for trombone -- 1/2 page
The cake walk - piano-vocal fragment -- 2 pages
Country life - for trombone -- 1 page
Smiles & tears - for trombone -- 1 page
Dear little love - piano-vocal fragment -- 2 pages
Hot tomale alley - for cornet in A -- 1 page
Katie Emmett March - for cello -- 1 page
Mahooney's days - for 2nd violin & viola -- 2 pages
Medley selection of Southern plantation songs - for violin -- 1 page
Tandem 2-step - for 1st violin -- 1 page [torn]
Every night in central park - 1st violin -- 1 page [torn]
Unidentified - for 1st violin & viola -- 2 pages
